description: Image on one side, heading + text + CTA on the other. Image position is configurable.

settings.label: Body
settings.default: PNEC is a community-based emergency preparedness organization serving the neighborhoods of Poway, CA.

settings.heading: Founded in 2003 by Poway neighbors.
settings.body:
PNEC was started after the 2003 Cedar Fire by residents who realized the only people on their block during an emergency would be each other. Today we connect 3,200+ households across 14 neighborhoods.

Every coordinator, instructor, and board member is a volunteer.
settings.image_alt: Group photo of PNEC volunteers at a community event

settings.heading: Three days. That's the gap.
settings.body:
FEMA and Cal OES recommend every household keep 72 hours of supplies — food, water, medications, cash. After a major event, that's how long it can take help to reach you.

One in three Poway households loses power, water, or shelter for some part of the year. PNEC closes the gap.

settings.heading: Join 100+ block volunteers.
settings.body:
Block coordinators check in on their neighbors during outages, share preparedness tips at meetings, and help keep the household roster current. About 4 hours a year.

No prior emergency experience needed — we train you.
settings.image_alt: Block coordinator chatting with neighbors on a porch
